13C and 15N chemical shifts of the membrane-reconstituted subunit c-ring of

saveframes.name: citation_1
tags:
Structural analysis of the membrane-reconstituted subunit c-ring of E. coli 
H+-ATP synthase by solid-state NMR

saveframes.name: assembly
tags: subunit c-ring of FoF1 ATP synthase (EFoc-ring)
From E. coli. The EFoc oligomer takes on a ring structure.

data:
Backbone and sidechain 13C and 15N Chemical Shift Assignments of H+-ATP synthase
subunit c in the solid state
Subunit c oligomer of the E.coli ATP Synthase

saveframes.name: sample_1
tags:
The EFoc is in DMPC-d54 bilayers. Protein/lipid molar ratio is 1:40.
The sample is hydrated. De-ionized water/total ratio is 10-50%.

saveframes.name: sample_2
tags: The EFoc is in DMPC-d54 bilayers. Protein/lipid molar ratio is 1:40.

data: Felix NMR Inc., San Diego, CA

saveframes.name: reference_1
tags:
The 13C chemical shift was referenced to DSS by using the methine carbon signal
of adamantane under MAS at 40.5 ppm relative to DSS.     
The 15N chemical shift was referenced to that of liquid NH3 deduced from the 13C
chemical shift using the 15N/13C ratio following the IUPAC recommendation.

saveframes.name: chemical_shift_1
tags: In the ring structure, the side chain of essential Asp61 takes on the COOH state.
